blocks.content: We got to the corner where the bus routes split. She had to go left. I was going right. We both stopped at the same time.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: You know what I liked most about it?

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: You were just — here. Like you weren't performing anything. You just showed up.

blocks.speaker: Crystal
blocks.content: Keep working on it. It's good.

blocks.prompt: The corner. She's about to turn. You have about three seconds.

options.label: I lean in. Just a little. Close enough that she can choose.
"See you Monday." She smiles. She turns. You watch her go.

blocks.speaker: Jonathan
blocks.content: you should go talk to her

blocks.speaker: Larry
blocks.content: She came back for you j, not me.
I watched him walk over to the bleachers like it was nothing. Like the stakes were zero. She looked up from her notebook and smiled before he even got to the bottom step.
I stood at the sideline with my bag on my shoulder. Some people just made it look easy. Not because it was easy — because they decided it was going to happen before they started walking.
I was still learning that part.
